metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the output shape the `run_case` code-review arm produces (individual-prompt `{findings:[...]}` vs. full-gate `{verdict:BLOCK|PASS, blocking:[...]}`) and confirm the new `code_review_*` scorers handle that exact shape — particularly that a BLOCK verdict (not FAIL) is correctly recognised as 'fired'.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify the output shape the new `run_case` code-review arm will produce, creating a latent mechanism mismatch: the full four-pass gate emits `{verdict: 'BLOCK'|'PASS', blocking:[...], advisory:[...]}`, but `eval_scorers._fired()` only recognises `verdict == 'FAIL'` (the completion-verifier vocabulary) — so a BLOCK verdict would silently count as 'not fired', making SEEDED-DEFECT recall cases vacuously pass.
findings.location: AC1 / eval_solver.py (new code-review arm) / eval_scorers.py (new scorers)
findings.suggested_fix: If the arm runs individual prompts (the plan-review criterion arm pattern), it returns `{findings:[...]}` and existing scorers work unchanged. State this explicitly. If it runs the full gate, extend `_fired` to also match `verdict == 'BLOCK'` and add `'block'` to `FIRE_EXPECTS` so dataset validation accepts it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in Scope whether the new run_case arm runs (a) the full code-review gate YAML via the workflow executor with a diff fixture, (b) individual prompt steps in isolation (base-only, verify-only), or (c) a new thin wrapper; name the relevant files (workflow/gates/code-review.yaml, code_review/workflow_ops.py, code_review/assemble.py) if they are in scope.
findings.finding: The plan says 'Add a run_case arm that executes these prompts' for code-review-base, code-review-verify, and overlay ids, but the code-review gate is a multi-step workflow (assemble_diff → overlay_union → per-overlay agent steps → merge_findings → verify → decide → coach), defined in src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/code-review.yaml. This is a fundamentally different execution model from the existing three single-reviewer arms (completion-verifier, ticket-quality, spec-alignment), each of which calls one operation function. The plan does not name the gate YAML, does not name assemble_diff_context or any workflow step as a scope target, and does not specify whether the new arm runs the full gate, a single-step slice (just the base reviewer), or a new thin wrapper. This leaves the behavioral boundary of the new arm ambiguous.
findings.location: What section ('Add a run_case arm that executes these prompts') / Scope section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the plan (or in the Scope section) whether the new `run_case` code-review arm returns the raw `code_review_verdict` shape or the shim-translated `review_result` shape, and confirm which existing scorers (or new `code_review_*` scorers) cover that shape without vacuous pass.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that 'code_review_* gating scorers' must be registered (AC1), but does not resolve which output shape the new run_case arm will return to those scorers. The code-review gate's raw output (code_review_verdict from produce_code_review_verdict) carries a different top-level structure (blocking/advisory/coaching/coverage sub-dicts) than the review_result shape (top-level 'findings' list) that the existing generic scorers (_recall, _no_fire, _schema_review_result) are wired for. The shim translates between the two shapes, but whether the new eval arm calls the shim or the raw gate is unspecified — leaving the scorer reusability claim unverified.
findings.location: AC1 / Scope: eval_solver.py (new code-review arm), eval_scorers.py (new scorers)
findings.suggested_fix: Have the new arm call `review_code(diff_text=..., repo_root=root, runner=runner)` (the shim), which returns a `review_result` dict the existing `_recall`/`_no_fire`/`_schema_review_result` scorers already handle. Then the 'new code_review_* scorer' requirement in AC1 reduces to registering alias names in REGISTRY pointing to those same functions — clarify this in the plan so implementers don't build redundant scorers.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define exactly what the new `code_review_*` gating scorer(s) must validate (e.g., `emits_valid_code_review_verdict` checking `verdict ∈ {BLOCK,PASS}`, `BLOCK⇔blocking non-empty`, `advisory` is a list) so an implementer knows what to add to eval_scorers.REGISTRY beyond the existing generic scorers.
findings.finding: The plan's AC#1 mandates '>=1 code_review_* gating scorer registered in eval_scorers.py' but never defines what such a scorer validates that the already-registered generic scorers (emits_valid_review_result, recall_on_seeded_defects, no_fire_on_good_cases) don't already cover for code-review outputs. An implementer cannot determine what to add.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a subsection under AC#1: 'The code_review_verdict scorer must check: (a) verdict ∈ {BLOCK, PASS}; (b) BLOCK ⇔ len(blocking) > 0; (c) advisory is a list; (d) coverage key present. Register as `emits_valid_code_review_verdict` in REGISTRY.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the code-review eval-case input protocol: name the case fields (e.g., `diff_text`, `changed_files`, `files`), define how `cfg`/`LLMConfig` is supplied to `produce_code_review_verdict` from within `run_case`, and confirm whether the arm calls `produce_code_review_verdict` or drives `run_workflow` directly (with the FakeRunner offline seam).
findings.finding: The plan says 'Add a run_case arm that executes these prompts' (code-review-base, code-review-verify, overlay ids) but never defines the eval-case input protocol for code-review cases: which fields a dataset case carries (diff_text? changed_files? files map?), how run_case threads an LLMConfig into produce_code_review_verdict (which requires `cfg` as its first positional arg, absent from run_case's signature), or whether the arm calls produce_code_review_verdict or run_workflow directly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reground AC4's nit-framing baseline expectations in independently specified correct behavior (e.g., explicit human-labeled expected outputs in the fixture file) rather than 'passes on the current gate,' to avoid snapshotting a potentially miscalibrated oracle.
findings.finding: AC4 specifies that the nit-framing experiment's no-fire/recall expectations must 'pass on the current gate as baseline,' making the oracle the current (possibly miscalibrated) gate output rather than independently specified correct behavior. If the current gate is wrong on nit-framing cases, this locks in the wrong expectation — the classic self-authored-oracle / change-detector anti-pattern the rubric flags as MAJOR.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4: 'its no-fire/recall expectations pass on the current gate as baseline'
findings.suggested_fix: In the fixture for the nit-framing experiment, hard-code the expected scorer outcome (FIRE or NO-FIRE) based on human judgment of the dso incident, independent of what the current gate produces. AC4 should read: 'fixture specifies human-labeled expected outcomes; the current gate must match those labels' — not 'expectations pass on the current gate.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that the new run_case code-review arm uses the existing diff_text= seam in assemble_diff_context (i.e. eval cases carry a diff_text field, not files + git-range), rather than reusing the ticket-oriented case_store scaffolding that calls rebar.init_repo.
findings.finding: The plan proposes a new run_case arm for code-review prompts but the existing case_store infrastructure stands up a ticket store (rebar.init_repo), while the code-review gate consumes a diff (diff_text + changed_files via assemble_diff_context). Cargo-culting the ticket-fixture pattern onto diff-based eval cases ignores the existing offline seam (diff_text=) and would force a mis-fit scaffolding.
findings.location: What section ('new code-review arm') / src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py case_store / src/rebar/llm/code_review/assemble.py assemble_diff_context
findings.suggested_fix: In the new run_case arm for 'code-review-base' / overlay ids / 'code-review-verify', call produce_code_review_verdict(cfg, diff_text=case['diff_text'], changed_files=case.get('changed_files'), enabled=True, runner=runner) directly — no case_store / rebar.init_repo needed. Fixture cases in the eval spec carry a diff_text: key (a minimal unified diff string) instead of files:.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether the new code-review gating scorers include a schema scorer validating the CodeReviewResult contract fields ('recommend_overlays', 'coverage_gaps', 'summary') from code_review/contracts.py, and add one if not planned.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the REGISTRY 'has no scorer validating a code_review_* output', implying new code-review-specific scorers are needed. However, the existing generic scorers (_recall/_no_fire via 'recall_on_seeded_defects'/'no_fire_on_good_cases') already handle fire/no-fire semantics for any dict with a 'findings' key — but there is NO schema scorer for the code-review-specific output shape (which adds 'recommend_overlays', 'coverage_gaps', and 'summary' fields defined in contracts.py). This specific gap is unmentioned and unverified in the plan.
findings.location: What section: 'eval_scorers.py REGISTRY has no scorer validating a code_review_* output. Add ... register code-review gating scorers'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the 'nit-framing experiment' inline in the plan (or link to the referenced story): specify the prompt variant, at least one representative input fixture, and the expected no-fire/recall outcome so a fixture + scorer assertion can be authored without clarification.
findings.finding: The 'nit-framing experiment' referenced in AC4 is never defined anywhere in the plan — no description of what the experiment is, what inputs it uses, or what its expected outputs are. An executing agent cannot author a fixture and scorer assertion for an unnamed/undefined experiment without stopping to ask.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sub-bullet under AC4 such as: 'nit-framing experiment = code-review-base with severity labels replaced by nit/suggestion/blocker framing; fixture: [link or inline snippet]; expected: no-fire on style-only diff, flag on tautological-test diff.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit implementation step and named output file (e.g. `eval_specs/nit_framing_experiment.yaml`) for the nit-framing eval case so AC4 maps to at least one concrete deliverable in the What/Scope sections.
findings.finding: AC4 has no corresponding implementation step in the What or Scope sections; 'build eval specs for base/overlays/verify' covers ACs 2–3, but the nit-framing experiment fixture is not listed as a deliverable anywhere in the plan body, leaving AC4 unreachable by the described work.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a description of the nit-framing experiment to the What or Scope section (what it targets, which cases count as no-fire vs. recall, what fixture/assertion is expected) so AC-4 maps to described work and is independently verifiable.
findings.finding: AC-4 references 'the nit-framing experiment' as a committed eval case with fixture + scorer assertion and specific no-fire/recall baseline expectations, but the plan body (What, Scope, Why) never describes this experiment — its cases, what 'nit-framing' means, which prompt/detector it targets, or what fixtures must exist. This is an orphan criterion with no covering description.
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, add a bullet under the eval spec families (or as a third family): 'Nit-framing experiment: [describe the prompt variation being tested], encoded as fixture(s) in eval_specs/ with scorer assertions verifying [specific no-fire / recall expectations].'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify whether the nit-framing experiment has been conducted and its fixture committed; if not, either create and commit the fixture + scorer assertion as part of this story's deliverables, or rewrite AC-4 as a future deliverable ('will encode') with a separate tracking ticket.
findings.finding: AC-4 asserts 'The nit-framing experiment is encoded as a committed eval case (fixture + scorer assertion)' as a present-tense acceptance criterion, but no such committed eval case exists anywhere in the codebase — no file in eval_specs/, code_review/, or evals/ contains a 'nit-framing' fixture, case id, or scorer assertion referencing a nit-framing experiment.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC-4: 'The nit-framing experiment is encoded as a committed eval case'
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite AC-4 as: 'A nit-framing eval case (fixture + scorer assertion encoding its no-fire/recall expectations) is committed to eval_specs/ as part of this story' — and include the fixture creation in scope.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the 'nit-framing experiment' in the plan: describe what prompt behavior is being tested (e.g., whether framing a finding as a 'nit' suppresses it), what the fixture diff/case looks like, and what the scorer assertion checks (fire vs. no-fire, or a specific output field value).
findings.finding: AC#4 references 'the nit-framing experiment' as something to 'encode as a committed eval case (fixture + scorer assertion)' — but 'nit-framing' is undefined anywhere in the codebase (zero grep hits) and is never explained in the plan. An implementer cannot author the fixture or its scorer assertion.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive rationale in the plan for the chosen `run_case` execution level (individual-prompt isolation vs. full four-pass workflow), explaining why that level fits the eval's measurement goal and codebase seams.
findings.finding: The plan states no positive rationale for why the code-review eval arm should run at individual-prompt granularity (base/verify/overlay in isolation) rather than via the full four-pass workflow — a non-trivial design choice with different blast radius, realism, and scorer-shape implications.
findings.location: What / Scope section — 'eval_solver.run_case() has NO execution arm for code-review prompt ids'

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a 'Proving commands' section (or per-AC sub-bullet) specifying the exact command(s) — e.g. `pytest src/rebar/llm/evals/` and/or `rebar eval run --suite code-review` — that would produce green output and serve as the completion gate for each AC.
findings.finding: No concrete proving command (e.g. `pytest`, `rebar eval run`, or equivalent) is given for any acceptance criterion; the plan describes implementation steps but leaves verification implicit, making it impossible to confirm done-ness without inference.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all four ACs) and Scope section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Capture the deterministic-gate vs. llm-judge-report scorer contract and the eval_solver code-review arm interface in a CLAUDE.md update, inline doc block, or a brief ADR so future scorer authors understand what belongs in each tier.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel architectural decision — a two-tier scorer pattern (deterministic GATE + llm-judge REPORT) as a new pipeline stage in the eval harness — but does not include any ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc capture of this contract or the eval_solver ↔ eval_scorers interface for code-review prompt ids.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a section to CLAUDE.md (or a new docs/adrs/eval-code-review-scorer-contract.md) that defines: (1) what makes a scorer a 'gate' vs. a 'reporter', (2) the interface eval_solver.run_case() expects from a code-review scorer, and (3) how to add a new code-review prompt id to both files consistently.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify whether gating scorer thresholds (e.g., required recall fraction, allowed false-positive rate) are configurable per-spec-file or hardcoded, and if hardcoded, justify that binary pass/fail is sufficient for all planned case families.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that gating scorers will be deterministic but does not specify whether the pass/fail threshold for recall or no-fire cases is configurable or hardcoded, leaving the threshold evolution path unaddressed for scorers that may need tuning as the prompt corpus grows.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify error handling for the new LLM-call arm in eval_solver.py: define timeout value, at least one retry with backoff, and a distinct ERROR result state that scorers and CI can distinguish from a FAIL result.
findings.finding: The new run_case arm makes LLM calls but the plan specifies no error handling, timeout, retry, or graceful-degradation strategy for those calls. A hung or erroring LLM call will either block the eval run indefinitely or propagate an unhandled exception, with no indication to the operator of whether the failure was a prompt defect or an infra fault.
findings.location: Scope: eval_solver.py (new code-review arm) / AC line 1
findings.suggested_fix: Add an `eval_case_result` enum with PASS/FAIL/ERROR states. Wrap each LLM call in a try/except with a configurable timeout (e.g., 30 s), one retry on transient errors, and populate ERROR on unrecoverable failure. Log the exception with case_id so operators can triage.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether REGISTRY mutation is safe under parallel eval execution: either document that registration is module-level (import-time, before workers fork) and therefore safe, or add a note that parallel execution requires process-isolated registries.
findings.finding: The plan adds new scorers to the REGISTRY (shared module-level mutable state in eval_scorers.py) but does not address concurrency safety for parallel eval execution. If eval specs are run with a parallel test runner (e.g., pytest-xdist), multiple workers importing and registering scorers against the same registry object could produce races or duplicate registrations.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that AC-1's 'code_review_* scorer' means a NEW scorer with code-review-specific logic (e.g. validating `recommend_overlays` output shape or `overlay_id` provenance), not a prefixed alias of an existing generic scorer — if no code-review-specific logic is needed, reuse the existing REGISTRY names in the new eval specs instead of adding redundant aliases.
findings.finding: AC-1 requires '>=1 code_review_* gating scorer registered in eval_scorers.py', implying new prefixed scorer names, but the existing REGISTRY already contains the exact scorers needed for a findings-emitting code-review prompt (emits_valid_review_result, recall_on_seeded_defects, no_fire_on_good_cases, cites_real_paths). Adding code_review_-prefixed aliases that wrap the same underlying functions is NIH duplication within the registry.
findings.suggested_fix: If the only needed gate is 'the reviewer fired on seeded defects / did not fire on good diffs', reuse 'recall_on_seeded_defects' and 'no_fire_on_good_cases' directly in the new code-review eval specs. Reserve a new 'code_review_*' scorer entry only if there is logic specific to the code-review output schema (e.g. asserting that recommend_overlays is a valid list, or that a blocking finding carries a reviewer_id matching an OVERLAY_ID).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether the new code-review eval arm's fixture setup avoids a third _git_init copy (by using the diff_text= seam rather than a git range), OR — if a disposable repo is needed — factor _git_init into a shared internal helper at this point (Rule-of-Three is satisfied by the third call-site).
findings.finding: _git_init is already duplicated between eval_solver.py:27 and criterion_preview.py:369 (the latter explicitly names it 'mirrors eval_solver._git_init'). Adding a third call context inside a new code-review eval scaffold hits the Rule-of-Three trigger for abstraction, yet the plan proposes no shared helper.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `tests/unit/test_new_eval_specs.py` as a consumer that must pass; confirm new eval spec scorer names are either drawn from the existing REGISTRY or that scorer registration and spec authoring land in the same changeset.
findings.finding: test_new_eval_specs.py iterates over packaged eval specs and validates each via validate_eval_spec(strict=True), including checking that every deterministic scorer name is registered in eval_scorers.REGISTRY. Any new *.eval.yaml file added to src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/ is automatically picked up by this test. The plan names eval_specs/ as a scope target but does not enumerate test_new_eval_specs.py as a consumer that must pass.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
